sourceMovie.title: The Apartment
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Apartment for the same reason it broke through in 1960: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Billy Wilder keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: When Harry Met Sally...
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Apartment clicked for you, When Harry Met Sally... (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Apartment?
faq.answer: Try When Harry Met Sally..., Anora, Café Society—each shares DNA with The Apartment while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Apartment?
faq.answer: The Apartment (1960) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Apartment worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
